propertyIssueSummary.headline: Multiple moisture-related defects were observed across the flat, with repeated links to bathroom tile-joint issues, plumbing concerns, and one external wall crack condition.
keyPoints: Skirting-level dampness was reported in the hall, common bedroom, master bedroom, and kitchen.
The master bedroom wall shows dampness with efflorescence, which is more advanced than the other interior observations.
Leakage and seepage were also observed below the flat at the parking ceiling and on the common bathroom ceiling.
The source documents repeatedly point to open tile joints, bathroom-side hollowness, plumbing issues, and one external wall crack zone.

areaWiseObservations.observation: Observed dampness at the skirting level of Hall of Flat No. 103. Supporting exposed-side finding: Observed gaps between the tile joints of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103. Area sheet description: Hall Skirting level Dampness.

severity.label: Medium
severity.reasoning: Moisture signs and open tile joints point to a recurring issue that needs repair before it spreads.

recommendedActions: Open and re-grout the affected tile joints, then check for loose or hollow tiles.
Allow the area to dry after repairs, remove damaged finish layers, and repaint only after moisture levels stabilize.

areaWiseObservations.observation: Observed dampness & efflorescence on the wall surface of Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103. Supporting exposed-side finding: Observed cracks on the External wall of building near Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103. Area sheet description: Master bedroom wall damness.
areaWiseObservations.probableRootCause: External wall crack and Duct Issue

severity.label: High
severity.reasoning: Visible salt deposits or cracking suggest the moisture issue has been present long enough to affect surfaces. The inspection checklist also rates the external wall condition as moderate.

recommendedActions: Seal external wall cracks and repair any gaps around service penetrations to reduce water ingress.
Allow the area to dry after repairs, remove damaged finish layers, and repaint only after moisture levels stabilize.

areaWiseObservations.observation: Observed leakage at the Parking ceiling below Flat No. 103. Supporting exposed-side finding: Observed plumbing issue & gaps between the tile joints of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103. Area sheet description: Parking Area seepage.
areaWiseObservations.probableRootCause: Common Bathroom tile hollowness and plumbing issue

severity.label: High
severity.reasoning: Leakage or seepage indicates active moisture movement that can spread and worsen nearby finishes.

recommendedActions: Open and re-grout the affected tile joints, then check for loose or hollow tiles.
Inspect concealed and exposed plumbing lines near the affected area and repair any leaking joints.

additionalNotes: Thermal pages provide hotspot and coldspot values but do not name the room or area directly.
The final report keeps thermal images attached as supporting evidence and marks the page-to-area match as inferred where necessary.
No facts have been added beyond the supplied documents; where clarity is missing, the report says so explicitly.
missingOrUnclearInformation: Room labels inside the thermal report: Not Available
Exact image-to-room mapping inside the thermal report: Not Available
conflicts: No direct contradictions were found, but some moisture areas have more than one possible contributing cause in the inspection notes.

checklistFlags: Leakage due to concealed plumbing Yes
Leakage due to damage in Nahani trap/Brickbat coba under tile flooring Yes
Gaps/Blackish dirt Observed in tile joints Yes
Gaps around Nahani Trap Joints Yes
Are there any major or minor cracks observed over external surface? Moderate
Algae fungus and Moss observed on external wall? Moderate
